questions.q: In which year was the 42nd Amendment to the Constitution passed?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment was passed in 1976.

questions.q: The 42nd Amendment is often given which nickname because of its sweeping changes?
questions.explanation: Because of its wide-ranging changes, the 42nd Amendment is often called the 'Mini-Constitution'.

questions.q: Which two words did the 42nd Amendment add to the Preamble of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment added the words 'socialist' and 'secular' to the Preamble.

questions.q: The 42nd Amendment introduced which new category of provisions into the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment introduced the Fundamental Duties into the Constitution.

questions.q: The 42nd Amendment made changes to which document?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment amended the Constitution of India.

questions.q: An 'amendment' to the Constitution is best described as:
options: A formal change to its provisions
questions.explanation: An amendment is a formal change to the provisions of the Constitution, as the 42nd Amendment made.

questions.q: The Preamble, which the 42nd Amendment altered, is described as the:
questions.explanation: The Preamble is the introductory statement of the Constitution, which the 42nd Amendment modified.

questions.q: The 42nd Amendment was enacted during which decade?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment was enacted in 1976, during the 1970s.

questions.q: The Fundamental Duties introduced by the 42nd Amendment are obligations of which group?
questions.explanation: The Fundamental Duties list moral obligations of citizens.

questions.q: The 42nd Amendment is classified in this deck as which type of entry?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment is presented as a landmark event in India's constitutional history.

questions.q: The 42nd Amendment added the Fundamental Duties as which new part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Fundamental Duties were added as Part IV-A by the 42nd Amendment.

questions.q: Before the 42nd Amendment added them, India's Constitution had originally included no formal list of which of the following?
questions.explanation: The original Constitution had Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles but no Fundamental Duties until the 42nd Amendment.

questions.q: The procedure used to pass the 42nd Amendment is laid down in which article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 368 lays down the procedure for amending the Constitution, used for the 42nd Amendment.

questions.q: After the words added by the 42nd Amendment, the Preamble describes India as a sovereign, socialist, secular, democratic:
questions.explanation: The amended Preamble describes India as a sovereign, socialist, secular, democratic republic.

questions.q: The 42nd Amendment is called the 'Mini-Constitution' chiefly because it:
options: Was printed in a small booklet
Changed many parts of the Constitution at once
questions.explanation: It earned the nickname by making wide-ranging changes to many parts of the Constitution in a single amendment.

questions.q: The original number of Fundamental Duties introduced by the 42nd Amendment was:
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment originally introduced ten Fundamental Duties; an eleventh was added later by the 86th Amendment.

questions.q: Which examples of obligations are among the Fundamental Duties introduced by the 42nd Amendment?
options: Respecting the national flag and anthem
questions.explanation: The Fundamental Duties include respecting the Constitution, the national flag and the anthem.

questions.q: The 42nd Amendment was a constitutional amendment, which under Article 368 generally requires what in Parliament?
options: A simple voice vote with no count
A unanimous vote of all members
questions.explanation: Amendments under Article 368 generally require a special majority in Parliament.

questions.q: The word 'secular', added by the 42nd Amendment, reflects which constitutional principle?
options: The state has an official religion
The state treats all religions equally
questions.explanation: Secularism in India means the state treats all religions equally and has no official religion.

questions.q: The 42nd Amendment is closely associated with which two additions that are now well known features of the Constitution?
options: The national flag and the anthem
The Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha
The words 'socialist'/'secular' and the Fundamental Duties
questions.explanation: Its best-known additions are the words 'socialist' and 'secular' in the Preamble and the Fundamental Duties.

questions.q: The Fundamental Duties introduced by the 42nd Amendment also include an obligation to protect which of the following?
questions.explanation: The Fundamental Duties include protecting the natural environment.

questions.q: The committee whose recommendations informed the Fundamental Duties added by the 42nd Amendment was chaired by which person?
questions.explanation: The Swaran Singh Committee, chaired by Sardar Swaran Singh, recommended including Fundamental Duties.

questions.q: Some provisions of the 42nd Amendment were later struck down on the ground that they violated the basic structure, as held in which 1980 case?
questions.explanation: In the Minerva Mills case (1980) the Supreme Court struck down parts of the 42nd Amendment as violating the basic structure.

questions.q: The eleventh Fundamental Duty, supplementing the ten added by the 42nd Amendment, was inserted by which later amendment relating to education?
questions.explanation: The 86th Amendment added an eleventh Fundamental Duty concerning education of children.

questions.q: Some of the more far-reaching changes made by the 42nd Amendment were subsequently modified by which amendment passed in 1978?
questions.explanation: The 44th Amendment of 1978 reversed or modified several changes made by the 42nd Amendment.

questions.q: Besides the Preamble and duties, the 42nd Amendment also sought to adjust the relationship between the legislature and which other organ regarding constitutional review?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment included provisions affecting the powers of the judiciary in reviewing constitutional matters.

questions.q: The Fundamental Duties added by the 42nd Amendment were modelled in spirit on duties found in the constitutional traditions of which kind of comparison the drafters considered?
options: Other constitutions that listed citizens' duties
questions.explanation: The idea of listing citizens' duties drew on the example of other constitutions that included such duties.

questions.q: The 42nd Amendment is formally titled the Constitution (Forty-second Amendment) Act of which year?
questions.explanation: Its formal title is the Constitution (Forty-second Amendment) Act, 1976.

questions.q: The 42nd Amendment also added words to the Preamble describing the unity and integrity of the nation, joining 'unity' with which term?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment changed 'unity of the Nation' to 'unity and integrity of the Nation' in the Preamble.

questions.q: Which pairing correctly matches each constitutional change to the amendment that made it?
options: 'Secular' added by the 61st Amendment; voting age lowered by the 42nd Amendment
'Secular' added by the 42nd Amendment; voting age lowered by the 61st Amendment
Both changes made by the 42nd Amendment
Both changes made by the 61st Amendment
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment added 'secular' to the Preamble, while the voting age was lowered from 21 to 18 by the 61st Amendment.
